overall.description: First-person exploration of a destroyed classical building in a game engine, likely set in post-apocalyptic Paris. The player walks along the weathered stone facade, examines the ornate columned portico, and emerges into an open plaza featuring abstract geometric sculptures with the Eiffel Tower visible in the distance.

segments.scene_description: The segment shows a slow walk along the side of a severely damaged classical stone building. Rubble and debris cover the ground, with sandbag piles visible in the foreground. The building's weathered facade with ornate window frames dominates the left side of the frame under bright midday sun.

segments.scene_description: The camera turns to face the grand portico of the damaged building, revealing tall stone columns and an ornate pediment. The collapsed roof section is visible above the structure. The scene transitions from the side facade to the formal entrance as the player explores the architectural details.

segments.scene_description: The scene opens into a wide plaza with abstract geometric sculptures dominating the center. The ground transitions to smooth paving stones, and the Eiffel Tower becomes visible in the distant left background. The damaged building falls behind as the camera moves into the open space under a clear blue sky.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot follows a continuous camera movement from the claustrophobic ruins of a classical facade into a wide, open urban plaza. It begins with a slow walk along a severely damaged stone building, passing piles of sandbags and rubble under a bright midday sun. As the camera turns, the perspective shifts from the weathered side wall to the grand portico, revealing towering stone columns and a collapsed roof section. The scene then opens into a vast plaza of smooth paving stones, where the damaged architecture falls behind to make room for abstract geometric sculptures. Finally, the view expands under a clear blue sky to reveal the Eiffel Tower standing in the distant background.
